title: Cerasus × hisauchiana
taxonomy: Cerasus × hisauchiana is a hybrid cherry species within the genus Cerasus, belonging to the family Rosaceae. This classification places it among the stone fruits, sharing characteristics with other members of the rose family. As a hybrid, Cerasus × hisauchiana combines genetic traits from its parent species, contributing to its unique characteristics. intro: Cerasus × hisauchiana, also known as Japanese apricot or Japanese plum, is a hybrid deciduous shrub or small tree species belonging to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cerasus × hisauchiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, this plant is often prepared as a decoction or made into a tea, as well as being used in various herbal formulas to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of the common cold.
